text.title: Build vs Buy Decision Framework Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team builds an internal queue 'because Kafka is fashionable' when SQS / RabbitMQ fits.
antipattern.fix: Map the capability on Wardley; require documented differentiation before approving Build.

antipattern.symptom: Decision uses only year-1 numbers; Buy looks cheap but year-3 license + lock-in dominate.
antipattern.fix: Force 3-year TCO with maintenance + infra + exit cost.

antipattern.symptom: Vendor doubles price at renewal; team cannot migrate in time.
antipattern.fix: Require exit-plan section in every Buy ADR with data export format + alternative provider list.

antipattern.symptom: Pilot deemed successful by intuition; production rollout reveals operational gaps.
antipattern.fix: Require pass/fail criteria pre-pilot; sign-off only when criteria met.
